identified_role: startup/bootstrap configuration routine with direct JS5 RSA key consumption

evidence.observation: loads ASCII string "runescape" into a newly initialized object
loads global slot for embedded js5.modulus object (0x140E58C18)
loads global slot for shared exponent object (0x140E58C10)
calls helper 0x14015D330 after staging endpoint string, js5 modulus, exponent, and another context pointer on the stack
stores constant 0x3B3 (947 decimal) into a staged field in the same object-building path
stores the resulting object into [rsi+0x18D10]/[rsi+0x18D18], indicating the constructed state is retained by the client context

current_conclusion: The patched JS5 modulus is consumed directly in a startup/bootstrap routine rather than sitting unused in .rdata.
That routine also consumes the shared exponent and stages network-ish endpoint strings, which is consistent with JS5 bootstrap or cache-channel configuration.
The login modulus static initializer is identified, but a comparable direct .text consumer has not yet been isolated from this pass.
next_leads: Trace helper 0x14015D330 to determine the exact object or protocol payload built from endpoint + js5 key pair.
Resolve the call chain from function 0x140020240 upward to learn when during client startup this bootstrap occurs.
Find downstream or indirect consumers of the login modulus global 0x140E58C08.
